item.title: challenge: Can teach you html in one blog?
item.description:
HTML

HTML, Which stands for (HyperText Markup Language), is the standard markup language for documents designed to be displayed in a web browser. It can be assisted by technologies such as Cascading Style Sheets (CSS) and scripting languages such as JavaScript(JS). Wikipedia

but How exactly are you gonna teach me HTML?

HTML is Really simple and it’s not hard to start a verrry little webpage of your, However!, HTML Alone will leave you with unconfigured unstyled, very bad looking webpage, so you should use CSS to help you giving your pages a single formed style.

Awesome!, what are the Requirments to start learning

Actually…

  • an editor (vscode, vim, notepad..)

  • a Supported Browser.

  • a head with brain inside it.

ok, how HTML Code is supposed to look like?

kind of like this…

<!DOCTYPE html>

<head>
    <title>Document</title>

</head>

<body>

    <p>This is where you are supposed to write!</p>

</body>


</html>

this is the absloute basic of website which only shows the text in between

<p></p>

What Each part of this means?

Let’s start with each one from top to bottom..

<!DOCTYPE html>

this line lets the browser know that it’s opening an HTML file and thus rendering it as a webpage, it’s needed in any webpage.

<head>
</head>

this requires a bit of explanation… as we discussed before, using HTML without CSS will leave us with nothing but bad styled text webpage and thus we need css and javascript, but how do we include them? this is where <head></head> comes in handy! it allows us to link css and js files to our html and thus giving it Style and functionality. this topic is for another blog but let’s keep going with the other commands

<body></body>

this one contain all the visible elements in the webpage wheter hyperlinks,texts,articles..

<p></p>

this command let us write text to our webpage otherwise how can we write blogs :D.

<title></title>

this one give the website a title, when your page loads check this..

<a href="https://www.google.com/">Google</a>

this one works similar to <p></p> except this one leads to a webpage when clicked.

is this enough to begin with?

i can’t say it’s alot but it should help you understand HTML and give you a clue about how website works combining the knowledge with css will give you a fine website like mine;(which is generated using blop)!

Finally

i Think this isn’t too much to learn from this blog, you might have spent 10 mins at most reading it but i spent 2 hours writing and constructing, Maybe, i can give this blog another re-write but as of now here is learn HTML in one blog.

item.title: code testing 1
item.description:
This blog is just about trying to write code in markdown

AVR Assembly

loop:
    inc r16
    jmp loop

C++

#include <iostream>
int main()
{
cout << "Hello, World!" << endl;
return 0;
}

python

print("Hello")
for i in range(10)
print(i)

SQL

SELECT FROM DATABASE r1 from table e2 where NAME="Muhammed"

HTML


<head>
 <title>Blog!</title>
 <meta charset="utf-8">
 <link rel="alternate" type="application/atom+xml" title="RSS Feed" href="rss.xml">
 <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css">
 <link rel="shortcut icon" href="images/favicon.png">
</head>

to finish this i’m gonna post my neofetch/s

--------------------------------------   muhammed@firebolt 
--------------------------------------   ----------------- 
--------------------------------------   OS: Bedrock Linux 0.7.25 Poki x86_64 
---\\\\\\-----------------------   Host: 80SY Lenovo V310-15ISK

this is it Thanks for keeping up! :D

item.title: Why can't everyone be into computer programming
item.description:
Why can’t everyone be a programmer (or could they)?

“Everyone should learn programming”

“programming is easy profit”

Maybe? but why Not?

Programming is a long journey and just like all other professions, it requires time, patience,passion,and a very big well to learn and for many reasons.

  • Programmers aren’t just coding, They are maintaining, Debugging, Testing, Creating better ideas for code or even optimizing it for newer Generations of computers.

  • Programming is not an easy task and that’s why programmers/Developers are sometimes paid better than some professions.

  • They spend so long time in front of the screen for most of the day Coding which is unhealthy in both physical and mental matter.

  • Understanding the Client’s demands and ideas is really necessary and should be studied carefully before beginning to code.

  • Program -> idea -> situations/problems -> flowchart -> code, not everyone can do this at least that what i saw from my classmate in university.

  • Not everyone can be a #1 dev from day one, it requires fine tuning every aspect of you knowledge in programming and then deploying it in it’s right way.

Finally please understand this; all the professions are equal, none are better nor are worse everyone is excel in thier own way, some are very awesome in cooking, others?, Artists,Enginners,Doctors,Lawyers and judges and also many others like sewers cleaning, Street cleaners, These Wonderful people are the reason why our society is clean, built, stable, and thriving; Everyone is Important in there own place with there own profession so we should respect everyone for better tommorow

item.title: about
item.description:
Welcome to My Blog, Here i will share My experience in many different topics and as this is the First Blog in this site i’m going to introduce myself

My name is Muhammed Sabbagh, a Computer Enginnering college student; currently First Year as of making this blog..

before that i learned many things espcially AVR microcontrollers, Proteus Simulation, and some programming languages like C#,C/C++,SQL

i have Discord Server for the sake of commenting at Blogs and Suggesting other Topics in mind Along with other Social Media platform.

Thanks for your time and have a nice day!
